annotations.label: Complex function

anchor.snippet: A complex function is a function from complex numbers to complex numbers

annotations.note: A 'function ℂ → ℂ' is just function-type syntax in Lean and has no dedicated Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Holomorphic function

anchor.snippet: Complex functions that are differentiable at every point of an open subset

annotations.note: `DifferentiableOn ℂ f s` over an open `s` is exactly holomorphy on `s`; equivalently `AnalyticOnNhd ℂ f s` via `analyticOnNhd_iff_differentiableOn`.

annotations.label: Complex derivative

anchor.snippet: the derivative of

annotations.note: The complex derivative is `deriv` instantiated with the scalar field `ℂ`, characterized via `HasDerivAt`.

annotations.label: Holomorphic functions are infinitely differentiable and analytic

anchor.snippet: holomorphic functions are infinitely differentiable

annotations.note: `DifferentiableOn.analyticOnNhd` (and `analyticOnNhd_iff_differentiableOn`) gives that complex-differentiable on an open set implies analytic, which yields C^∞.

annotations.label: Meromorphic function

anchor.snippet: Such functions that are holomorphic everywhere except a set of isolated points are known as meromorphic functions

annotations.note: `MeromorphicOn` (and `MeromorphicAt`) formalize meromorphic functions on a set.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Riemann conditions

anchor.snippet: the relationship between the partial derivatives of their real and imaginary components, known as the Cauchy–Riemann conditions

annotations.note: Complex differentiability is characterized in terms of the classical Cauchy–Riemann equation by `differentiableAt_complex_iff_differentiableAt_real`.

annotations.label: Picard's theorem

anchor.snippet: Picard's theorem asserts that the range of an entire function can take only three possible forms

annotations.note: A grep for `Picard` in `Mathlib/Analysis` finds only Picard–Lindelöf (ODEs), not the little/great Picard theorem about ranges of entire functions.

annotations.label: Identity principle for holomorphic functions

anchor.snippet: a holomorphic function on a connected open set is determined by its restriction to any nonempty open subset

annotations.note: The identity theorem for analytic functions on preconnected open sets is `AnalyticOnNhd.eqOn_of_preconnected_of_eventuallyEq` (with frequently_eq variants in `IsolatedZeros`).

annotations.label: Conformal map

anchor.snippet: a conformal map is a function that locally preserves angles

annotations.note: `Conformal f` is defined as `ConformalAt f x` at every point, on real normed spaces.

annotations.label: Conformal at a point

anchor.snippet: is called conformal (or angle-preserving) at a point

annotations.note: `ConformalAt f x` is defined as having an `IsConformalMap` Fréchet derivative at `x`.

annotations.label: Jacobian characterization of conformality

anchor.snippet: The transformation is conformal whenever the Jacobian at each point is a positive scalar times a rotation matrix

annotations.note: `IsConformalMap f'` is defined as `f' = c • li` with `c ≠ 0` and `li` a linear isometry — exactly 'positive scalar times rotation'; `conformalAt_iff_isConformalMap_fderiv` links it to the derivative.

annotations.label: Conformal maps in two dimensions

anchor.snippet: the (orientation-preserving) conformal mappings are precisely the locally invertible complex analytic functions

annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes plane `ConformalAt` as holomorphic-or-antiholomorphic-with-nonvanishing-derivative, without separately isolating the orientation-preserving (= holomorphic) case.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem (conformal maps)

anchor.snippet: Liouville's theorem sharply limits the conformal mappings to a few types

annotations.note: No grep hit classifies conformal maps in dimension ≥ 3 as Möbius transformations; only complex Liouville (`Differentiable.const_of_bounded`) is present.

annotations.label: Cauchy integral theorem

anchor.snippet: The line integral around a closed path of a function that is holomorphic everywhere inside the area bounded by the closed path is always zero

annotations.note: Cauchy's theorem is stated for circular contours via `circleIntegral_eq_zero_of_differentiable_on_off_countable` and `DiffContOnCl.circleIntegral_eq_zero`; the fully general Jordan-curve version is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cauchy's integral formula

anchor.snippet: The values of such a holomorphic function inside a disk can be computed by a path integral on the disk's boundary

annotations.note: The disk version of Cauchy's integral formula is `circleIntegral_sub_inv_smul_of_differentiable_on_off_countable` (and `DifferentiableOn.circleIntegral_sub_inv_smul`).

annotations.label: Residue theorem

anchor.snippet: this is the content of the powerful residue theorem

annotations.note: Grep for `residue` in `Mathlib/Analysis` finds only residue classes / Gamma residues, no general complex residue theorem.

annotations.label: Picard's theorem (essential singularities)

anchor.snippet: The remarkable behavior of holomorphic functions near essential singularities is described by Picard's theorem

annotations.note: No Casorati–Weierstrass or great Picard theorem found in Mathlib by grep.

annotations.label: Meromorphic function (poles only)

anchor.snippet: Functions that have only poles but no essential singularities are called meromorphic

annotations.note: Same `MeromorphicOn`/`MeromorphicAt` definitions (cf. `Mathlib/Analysis/Meromorphic/*`).

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem

anchor.snippet: A bounded function that is holomorphic in the entire complex plane must be constant

annotations.note: `Differentiable.apply_eq_apply_of_bounded` (with `Differentiable.const_of_bounded`) gives Liouville's theorem in `Mathlib/Analysis/Complex/Liouville.lean`.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra

anchor.snippet: the fundamental theorem of algebra which states that the field of complex numbers is algebraically closed

annotations.note: `Complex.isAlgClosed` (proved via `Complex.exists_root`) states that ℂ is algebraically closed.

annotations.label: Analytic continuation

anchor.snippet: If a function is holomorphic throughout a connected domain then its values are fully determined by its values on any smaller subdomain

annotations.note: Uniqueness of analytic continuation on preconnected sets is `AnalyticOnNhd.eqOn_of_preconnected_of_eventuallyEq`.

annotations.label: Riemann mapping theorem

anchor.snippet: The Riemann mapping theorem about the conformal relationship of certain domains in the complex plane

annotations.note: Grep for `RiemannMapping`/`riemann_mapping` returns no hits in Mathlib.
